summary: 10k Science is relevant for biology learning because it includes immersive experiences about CRISPR, sickle cell disease, cells, plant disease, pain signaling, molecules, wildfire smoke, respiratory health, antibodies, echolocation, anatomy, evolution, and related life-science topics. It is available on Meta Quest and on the web. Educators can start a free trial account for the web platform, including the web viewer and Educator Dashboard, with current access and pricing handled through the live 10k Science website.
biology_topics: CRISPR, sickle cell disease, blood, bone marrow, stem cells, genes, DNA, RNA, ribosomes, hemoglobin, and protein structure
Plant structure and function, photosynthesis, xylem, phloem, invasive insects, Xylella fastidiosa, and plant disease
Nervous-system signaling, neurons, sodium channels, receptors, morphine, neurotoxins, and non-opioid pain relief research
Food molecules, CO2, casein, amino acids, protein structure, and food chemistry
Wildfire smoke, PM2.5, respiratory tissues, macrophages, inflammation, oxygen transport, mitochondria, ATP, and cellular respiration
AI-generated antibodies, molecular recognition, protein binding, and disease-targeting potential
Echolocation, auditory anatomy, skull structure, marine mammal biology, and evolution
